entries.title: phased-dev
entries.description: Agent-driven phased development workflow built on a scope abstraction. Each scope declares its own pipeline; every pipeline opens with a research phase (prior art, feasibility, domain constraints) feeding the phases that follow. Shipped pipelines cover standard projects (research → brainstorm → engineering → plan → implement), design-heavy projects (research → brainstorm → ux → engineering → plan → implement, with full design language / component inventory / a11y contract), standard features (research → engineering → plan → implement), and design-heavy features (research → ux → engineering → plan → implement, for UX-led features like onboarding rewrites or editor modes). Bundles 10 unified slash commands (including rewind-phase and generate-ux-preview), role-separated subagents, per-task completion markers, per-feature status mirrors, and templates with phase gates and per-task execution logs.

entries.title: build-loop
entries.description: Multi-phase build orchestration with native debugging: assess, plan, execute, review, iterate. Plan-verify gate on Phase 2. Optional UI design gates. Native debug-loop, debugging-memory, and logging-tracer skills provide root-cause investigation without a bundled MCP server. Orchestrator owns when-to-fire; bundled skills own procedural detail; logging-tracer-bridge is an optional escalation hop to the standalone Coding Debugger supporting plugin for extended capability.

entries.title: larch
entries.description: Multi-agent workflow automation for Claude Code: issue-anchored `/design` (one direct-drafting flow; plan-review panel), `/bug` (consumer issue-filing helper), `/deps` issue dependency audits, and `/implement` (positional `<issue-N>`, fixed 7200s Step 2 timeout, optional `--merge`, `--forked`, `--draft`, `--no-admin-fallback`, `--no-logs-commit`, `--coder`, `--run-id`). `/implement` Preflight reads the `larch:plan` block from the issue body; `/implement` Step 5 code review always uses the unified strict panel internally (public `--panel` argv removed).

entries.title: claude-statusbar
entries.description: Lightweight Claude Code status-line monitor with switchable styles, themes, and slash commands. v3.15.1 fixes a background `git status` that could strand `.git/index.lock` and block your own commits, makes `cs config set warning_threshold`/`critical_threshold` actually drive the bar (they were silently ignored), and aligns the context-window colour across quota/no-quota modes (model name now uses the 70/85 context band, so ~35% context reads calm green instead of a false yellow); per-session no-quota detection under the shared daemon was also fixed. v3.14.1 lowers the projection red line to 85% (yellow now 70–84) so a 7d window heading for →99% reads red instead of merely warm. v3.14.0 colours the 5h/7d bars by where usage is HEADED, not where it is now: once an end-of-window projection exists, the bar fill, label, and reset clock take their severity from the projected % against the cap (green <80, yellow 80–99, red ≥100) while the fill length still shows current usage — so a 7d window at 24% but on track for →96% reads yellow instead of a falsely-healthy green (all three styles). It also fixes the →NN% projection reading far too low for the first ~15 min after a reset (it was seeded from the used=0 first tick and lagged); it now holds `→--` until MIN_ELAPSED, then seeds from the first trustworthy reading. v3.13.3–v3.13.5 fix the 5h/7d bars sticking at a stale high % after Anthropic re-baselines usage mid-window (e.g. weekly limit raised): the cross-session merge now accepts an official downward revision once the old reading goes unconfirmed for 120s, idle windows replaying hours-old rate_limits blobs (expired 5h reset) can no longer overwrite or re-confirm the shared reading, and the →NN% projection drops its old-denominator samples and relearns after a re-baseline instead of freezing for the rest of the window. v3.13.0 adds a ⚙ session-mode line — effort (low/medium/high/xhigh/max/ultracode/auto) · thinking · fast · output-style — with a distinct static colour gradient per effort tier. v3.12.0 adds an always-on end-of-window projection (→NN%, learned from your local work rhythm) + an at-risk `⚠eta` forecast after each reset timer, session lines on by default, and a faint on-bar version that shows a `↑` hint when a newer release is on PyPI. v3.10.0 adds an opt-in live-activity line (in-progress todo, active tool, completed-tool rollup), git ahead/behind + session duration/lines on the project line, running-subagent lines, an opt-in `bar_shimmer` starfield on the battery bars, and a self-hosted plugin marketplace — plus a reliability fix so auto-update actually runs in daemon mode (detached, non-blocking) and the cache countdown is now per-session-correct. v3.9.0 makes the `cache` countdown auto-detect the real prompt-cache TTL (5m vs 1h) from the transcript, fixing a ~55-minute early `cache COLD` for Claude subscription users (Claude Code requests the 1-hour TTL automatically). v3.7.0 defends against status-line hijacking: a new `cs --setup --project [PATH]` writes a project-level override so another tool stealing the global `~/.claude/settings.json` slot can't kick `cs` out of this project; when displacement is detected, the bar also surfaces a one-line warning. v3.6.0 makes daemon (fast) mode the default for `cs --setup` — under 1% CPU continuously instead of ~3% inline at refreshInterval=1; pass `--inline` to opt back. Requires the `cs` CLI from PyPI (`pip install claude-statusbar` or `uv tool install claude-statusbar`).

entries.title: antigravity
entries.description: Run the Antigravity CLI (Gemini) as a collaborating AI inside Claude Code, with intelligent model routing across the SDLC. Claude conducts — requirements, architecture, the hard 20%, verification, review — and routes deterministic, high-volume work (scaffolding, tests, first-pass review, migrations, web/Vertex AI Search) to Antigravity. Hybrid agentic engineering; lower token cost as a financial lever.

Plus a Claude Code Bash safety hook.

entries.title: quetzalcoatl
entries.description: Quetzalcoatl — Meaning-First AI Project OS. /Quetzalcoatl 한 번으로 의미검증→GarryTan Office Hours→3~5개 대안→Claude-GPT 교차검증→문서·대시보드→Ship→Retro 까지 운영하는 모델 중립형 스킬. v1.2.0: 지속 자율 실행·레이트리밋 회복력·문서 체계·Git 프로토콜·교차 에이전트/기기 핸드오프. v1.3.0: 앵커된 선호 처리(근거 게이트 override)·대안 비교 §4 승격. v1.4.0: 대시보드 HTML 미러·/dashboard 갱신(§7.2). v1.5.0: 강제 바인딩(규율→강제, 있을 때만)·증거기반 완료·capability matrix(외부 도구 역량). v1.6.0: /context 벤더-중립 휴대용 브리핑(§24).

entries.title: primereact-docs

entries.title: odoo-ai-skills
entries.description: The local-first verification & deploy gate for AI-written Odoo 17/18/19 changes — static indexes suggest; this verifies against the running instance. Read ground truth from the live instance first (fields, MRO/super chains, views, security, runtime flow, runtime state + exception post-mortem, reverse field impact, effective per-user/company security, module load state), build the smallest correct change, prove it with tests, and review it before merge — never guess. Layer-K discovery solves the cold-start problem — rank the live entrypoint surface (buttons/crons/automations/routes), sample the real cross-app flow as an Execution Surface Graph, measure hallucination reduction against a benchmark, and ENFORCE no-introspect-no-edit via a PreToolUse hook. Layer-I enforcement gates turn that evidence into proof: risk-based scenario test generation, dev-vs-prod environment parity, a static anti-pattern validator, enforced privacy redaction, an upgrade/migration harness, a deploy approval gate, a one-command evidence bundle (PR-comment verdict), and a bring-your-own-index claim verifier; plus a local Odoo developer-docs index (Layer J). No SaaS, no seats, no metadata leaves the box. Covers models, scaffolding, views, OWL, public web (controllers/website/portal), security, reports, data, migration, performance, deploy (incl. odoo.sh), and domain playbooks.

entries.title: effort-gate
entries.description: Universal EFFORT standing-order for Claude Code. Injects a full effort doctrine at SessionStart and re-asserts it every turn so the agent works each task to done-and-proven — refusing unnatural, slop, mean-reverting premature exits. In-generation governance, caveman-pattern, no post-turn detection.

entries.title: axhub
entries.description: Korean-first Claude Code plugin for axhub. onboarding/init/deploy/import/development/diagnosis/clarity/update 8개 스킬이 ax-hub-cli v0.20.0+ 표면(hidden plugin-support 그룹 + 공개 deploy verify·diagnose·update)을 직접 호출해요. import 는 기존 로컬 앱 가져오기를, diagnosis 는 배포 실패 원인 요약을 담당하고, preview-confirm 배포와 verify 기반 성공 선언을 제공해요.

entries.title: radical-pipelines
entries.description: An agent orchestrator that runs teams of agents autonomously through a pipeline of defined phases, where each phase produces concrete, inspectable artifacts.

entries.title: dev-loop
entries.description: A multi-agent software delivery loop coordinated through ticket state. PM, QA, Dev, Sweep, Reflect, Ops, Architect, and Communication cover product discovery, testing, implementation, hygiene, retrospectives, production watch, technical-health audits, and public article drafts. The optional senior/junior Dev split lets a stronger model design while a cheaper model implements. Coordination can run on Linear, a local file board, or the service hub with per-agent identity, a localhost web UI, documents, Lark/Slack channel, one-way Linear mirror, Codex/opencode portability, and a built-in scheduler that shells out to Claude or Codex without relying on /loop.

The user composes the workflow; sherpa offers the tools. Nothing runs unasked, nothing persists unless you call /persist. Project-agnostic; extend with project packs.
